name: Optimize NERVous GPT 5.5 low code-quality output per token

description: Baseline GPT 5.5 low quality-per-output-token benchmark for NERVous prompt/tooling surfaces

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline before changing prompt/tool descriptions; benchmark uses two representative NERVous orchestration/risk prompts with real openai-codex gpt-5.5 low model.

baseline_findings.risky_upload_api_plan: All rubric checks hit, but output was verbose at ~1067 estimated tokens.
baseline_findings.blocked_migration_triage: Missed SYNAPSE coordination note and triggered a penalty by quoting/echoing 'delete production data' phrasing while otherwise recommending pause/review.
primary_levers: reduce verbosity without dropping component coverage
make SYNAPSE coordination more salient in risk/blocker workflows
avoid repeating destructive-action phrase in a way that triggers unsafe-language penalty while still naming data-loss risk

description: Add concise workflow and AMYGDALA/SYNAPSE risk-note guidance

asi.hypothesis: Adding generic compact-checklist guidance and explicit AMYGDALA->SYNAPSE blocker notification should improve quality per token for low-thinking GPT by reducing verbosity and improving risk coordination.
asi.result: Primary improved from 63.65 to 67.38 by reducing output tokens ~11%, despite quality_score dropping 121->114.
learned: Concise checklist guidance reduced verbosity materially.
AMYGDALA/SYNAPSE guidance did not reliably cause SYNAPSE mention in outputs; both tasks still missed a coordination-note rubric at least once.
Blocked migration output still quoted the destructive phrase from the user, triggering unsafe-language penalty despite otherwise safe advice.
asi.next_action_hint: Try stronger but generic risk workflow wording: 'record SYNAPSE risk note' using exact term, and safe phrasing guidance around destructive operations; avoid bloating the API planning answer.
